item.title: BR west zone to lease 11 more trains to pvt operators, fare hike feared
item.description: Bangladesh Railway’s west zone is moving to hand over 11 more mail and local trains to private operators, citing revenue shortfalls......

item.title: Messi reaffirms enduring importance with hat-trick
item.description: With a dazzling hat-trick in Argentina’s 3-0 World Cup win over Algeria on Tuesday, the 38-year-old Lionel Messi vindicated coach Lionel Scaloni’s assertion on the eve of the ​match that he remains fundamental to the reigning champions......

item.title: PM vows tough action on money laundering
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday said his government will take a firm stance against money laundering abroad......

item.title: Jordan announces amnesty for undocumented Bangladeshis
item.description: Jordan has announced a general amnesty for undocumented migrant workers under certain conditions, according to a statement issued by the Bangladesh embassy in Amman on Wednesday......

item.title: Trial begins against ex-MP Hasnat Abdullah, 3 cops over cross-fire killings
item.description: The trial of fugitive former Awami League lawmaker Abdul Hasnat Abdullah and three former police officers began on Wednesday in a crimes against humanity case linked to the killing of two Bangladesh Nationalist Party activists in a reported ‘crossfire’ in 2015......

item.title: Further probe report submission deferred for 15th time
item.description: An additional chief metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Wednesday extended the deadline for submitting the further investigation report in the case of the killing of Inquilab Mancha convener Sharif Osman Hadi by 11 more days......

item.title: Coast Guard seizes Yaba worth Tk 1.5cr at Teknaf
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard in an operation seized 21,000 Yaba pills worth about Tk 1.5 crore at Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Wednesday......

item.title: Iranian tankers pass thru US blockade zone
item.description: The first tankers carrying Iranian oil exited a US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, a tracking website said on Wednesday, two days before Washington and Tehran launch talks on an agreed peace deal still scant on details — including Iran’s nuclear programme and the lifting of sanctions......

item.title: Prolonged outages leave villages power-starved
item.description: Parts of rural Bangladesh endured up to 20 hours of power outages on Tuesday as load shedding crossed the 3,000MW mark, once again exposing the rural-urban divide......

item.title: Banks incur Tk 1.37 lakh crore losses in 2025
item.description: Bangladesh’s banking sector suffered net loss of Tk 1.37 lakh crore in 2025 for the first time as a surge in defaulted loans, massive provisioning requirements and prolonged economic stress wiped out earnings and exposed weaknesses that had accumulated over the years......

item.title: Unrealistic revenue target may raise harassment of taxpayers: experts
item.description: Business leaders and economists on Wednesday warned that achieving the government’s revenue target for FY2026-27 may be difficult due to weak performance by the National Board of Revenue, cautioning that this could lead to increased taxpayer harassment without structural reforms......

item.title: S Korea keen to invest in electronics sectors
item.description: Appreciating Bangladesh’s rapid economic growth and investment-friendly environment, South Korean Ambassador Kim Ji-joon on Wednesday expressed his country’s interest in investing in Bangladesh’s electronics manufacturing and technology sectors......

item.title: Govt to procure 3 more LNG cargoes
item.description: The government on Wednesday decided to procure three cargoes of liquefied natural gas and fertiliser to meet the demand of energy items and agricultural ingredients......

item.title: Establishment of FTZs approved
item.description: The cabinet committee on economic affairs in a meeting on Wednesday agreed in principle with a proposal to establish the country’s first free trade zones in Chattogram......

item.title: BB agrees with 7-point demands over IBBL: clients
item.description: Bangladesh Bank governor Md Mostaqur Rahman has expressed agreement in principle with the seven-point demand put forward by the Islami Bank Sachetan Grahak Forum to restore corporate governance in Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC, the platform said on Wednesday......

item.title: Elevator importers seek restoration of capital machinery status
item.description: Bangladesh Elevator, Escalator and Lift Importers Association on Wednesday urged the government to reclassify elevators and escalators as capital machinery and rationalise the existing duty structure, arguing that the current tax regime is driving up housing and infrastructure costs while threatening the sector’s sustainability......

item.title: Stocks rebound after a brief correction
item.description: Stocks returned to an upward trend on Wednesday after a brief pause, supported by active buying from bargain hunters and sustained investor optimism over positive market expectations and hopes for a favourable resolution to the ongoing Middle East conflict......

item.title: Pubali Bank achieves strong business growth in 3 years: MD
item.description: Pubali Bank PLC managing director and chief executive officer Mohammad Ali on Tuesday said that, over the past three years, Pubali Bank achieved significant business growth and also created substantial value for its shareholders......

item.title: Delay in measles care protocol unacceptable
item.description: THE absence of a national clinical management protocol for physicians to deal with the highly infectious measles three months into an alarming outbreak is a serious lapse in public health preparedness. The outbreak has, as conservative official figures suggest, infected nearly 98,500 children and claimed 657 lives. Clinical guidelines are not bureaucratic formalities. They are......

item.title: Govt needs time-bound plan for NASSA workers’ pay
item.description: IRREGULARITIES and delays in the payment of worker wages have for long been common, but they are particularly acute in industrial units owned by individuals affiliated to the fallen Awami League regime. NASSA Group workers took to the streets and blocked a road at Tejgaon on June 16, demanding arrears and festival allowances, resulting in severe traffic congestion......

item.title: Saving haor rice before next flood
item.description: EVERY year, as the Boro harvest nears, anxiety returns to the haor basin. The fields turn golden, the crop is almost ready and national food security quietly depends on a narrow window of time. Then comes the familiar threat: heavy rainfall, upstream water flow, rising rivers, embankment failure, and the possibility that months of labour may be lost in a matter of hours......

item.title: When children can no longer trust their world
item.description: THE moral condition of a society is often revealed not by its wealth, infrastructure or political rhetoric but by how effectively it protects its most vulnerable members. A country may celebrate economic progress, expanded services and ambitious development goals, yet such achievements ring hollow if children cannot walk to school safely, play freely in their......

item.title: How Washington turned global crises into energy deals
item.description: IMAGINE an entity that stealthily wrecks its rivals’ warehouses, blocks their supply chains, and then attempts to sell the very goods the market is now clamouring for — yet claims to be a champion of global stability. This is the United States of America. Here, the warehouses are oil fields, the supply routes are maritime straits and the ‘goods’ are liquefied natural gas......

item.title: Experts urge teacher quality focus in edn budget
item.description: Educationists and researchers on Wednesday said the increased allocation for the education sector in the proposed national budget for the 2026-27 financial year should be spent on priority areas, particularly improving teachers’ quality through better training, pay and accountability......

item.title: Pay Tk 2 lakh to police shooting victim as interim compensation: HC
item.description: A High Court rule on Wednesday asked the home secretary and the police chief to explain why the 2019 shooting of a man in Munshiganj by the police that allegedly left him permanently disabled should not be declared illegal and without lawful authority......

item.title: AI not to make good schools obsolete: Cambridge official
item.description: Artificial intelligence is transforming education, but it will not make good schools obsolete as quality teaching and supportive learning communities remain essential, Cambridge International Education global managing director Rod Smith said on Tuesday......

item.title: Ronaldo fails to shine as DR Congo earn historic World Cup point
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo's record-equalling sixth World Cup got off to a disappointing start as the Democratic Republic of Congo secured their first ever point at the finals, drawing 1-1 with Portugal in their Group K match on Wednesday....

item.title: 72pc WASH budget goes to cities despite rural needs: study
item.description: Urban areas continue to receive nearly 72 per cent of the proposed allocation for the water, sanitation and hygiene sector despite significant service gaps in rural and hard-to-reach areas, according to a budget-tracking analysis released on Wednesday......

item.title: Bangladesh assumes UNHCR executive committee leadership
item.description: Bangladesh has assumed the chairmanship of the bureau of the executive committee of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees for the first time, marking a significant milestone in the country’s multilateral diplomacy......

item.title: Environment minister calls for restoration of rangeland, grassland
item.description: Environment, forest and climate change minister Abdul Awal Mintoo at a workshop in Dhaka on Wednesday called for the restoration of rangelands and grasslands......

item.title: Stalled services at Kushtia hospital to be restored soon: health minister
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar M Shakhawat Husain on Wednesday said that the suspended services at Kushtia 250-bed General Hospital, including closed wards and diagnostic facilities, would be restored soon through coordinated administrative measures......

item.title: BMU finance committee holds 119th meeting
item.description: The 119th meeting of Bangladesh Medical University’s finance committee was held at the Shaheed Dr Milton Hall of BMU in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: Tribandhon features three emerging artists
item.description: A six-day group painting exhibition styled ‘Tribandhon’ that showcased three emerging artists—Md Fariaz Emran, Niharika Awhona Barsaat and Surovi Akter—concluded on Wednesday at Alliance Française, Dhaka in the capital......

item.title: Jatra artistes should improve quality to earn appreciation: workshop
item.description: Speakers at a workshop in Dhaka on Wednesday said that jatra artistes should improve the quality of their production and performance to earn audience appreciation......

item.title: GAU professor Abdul Baset elected BSPST president
item.description: Professor Md Abdul Baset Mia, crop botanist, former dean of the faculty of agriculture and professor at Gazipur Agricultural University, has been elected president of the Bangladesh Society of Plant Science and Technology for the 2026–2028 term......

item.title: British Council launches CTC 2026 open call
item.description: The British Council has launched the 2026 cycle of its flagship Connections Through Culture grants programme......

item.title: Milestone College achieves success in NHSPC 2026
item.description: Milestone College Dakshinkhan has achieved success in the recent National High School Programming Competition......

item.title: G7 leaders applaud Iran ‘opportunity’, host AI chiefs
item.description: G7 leaders on Wednesday hailed the US-Iran deal to end the Middle East war as a ‘historic opportunity’ and hosted AI bosses for an unusual meeting to discuss greater protection for children online......

item.title: Listed criminals among 6 held for Adabar police attack
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion on Wednesday said it had arrested six people, including two listed criminals, in connection with the mugging of a bKash agent and the machete attack on two police officers in Dhaka’s Adabar area......

item.title: Prime minister Tarique Rahman leaves Dhaka for Moulvibazar via Sylhet
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman left Dhaka for Moulvibazar on Wednesday morning to attend two family card distribution programmes and a political meeting......
